summary: Backend regression tests for AUDY COOK NestJS backend. 25/25 tests passed (100%). Covered: public endpoints (products list + by slug + 404, menu, blog list + by slug + 404, resellers, whatsapp), admin auth (login success, wrong password 401, missing-fields 422, invalid email 422, /admin/me with and without Bearer), full CRUD for products / blog / menu (with auth-required negative tests), and checkout input validation (empty items, missing origin_url, unknown product_id -> 400, invalid quantity).
critical_code_review_comments: checkout.service.ts: Stripe is instantiated lazily but uses placeholder key 'sk_test_emergent' which is NOT a real Stripe secret. With the current Node Stripe SDK, any successful validation of items will then fail at session creation. Acceptable per request scope (validation only), but for real card payments user must set a real sk_test_... key.
auth.service.ts seedAdmin: bcrypt.hash format is bcryptjs ($2a$/$2b$ — fine). Idempotent: creates if missing else updates password if it differs. Good.
Validation pipe is global with whitelist + transform — DTO validation works as expected (422 / 400 returned).
All public list endpoints correctly strip MongoDB _id (verified in tests).
main.ts: enableCors is called twice (NestFactory.create({cors:true}) and app.enableCors). The second call overrides; harmless but redundant — recommend keeping only app.enableCors.
JWT does not currently appear to enforce expiration in tests (long-lived); consider adding expiresIn for production.

Admin auth uses Bearer JWT only (no httpOnly cookie / no brute-force lockout). Acceptable for an internal mini back-office, but the playbook checklist (httpOnly cookies, lockout after 5 fails) is NOT implemented. Flag only — was not in scope of this review.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seed (6 products, 5 menu items, 3 blog posts). Test CRUD created and cleaned up its own TEST_-prefixed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green on public + admin + checkout-validation endpoints. Stripe checkout session CREATION will fail at runtime because STRIPE_API_KEY is the Emergent placeholder 'sk_test_emergent' — only validation paths were tested. To test real Stripe success, swap STRIPE_API_KEY to a real sk_test_... key. Frontend was not tested in this iteration.
